mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, discount rates and financing costs decline, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts occur alongside rising inflation or if equity returns are negative despite a clear easing trend.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window but SPX 20d forward returns are negative in more than 60% of such cases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for 3 consecutive years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above the recent trend implies higher inflation pressure, which raises expected policy restraint and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if inflation spikes fail to precede weaker equity performance.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases above 0.3% are followed by positive SPX 20d returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the signal's hit rate is not better than 50% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress corporate revenue expectations and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The mechanism is strongest when unemployment is moving higher rather than merely level-shifting.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month UNRATE increases above 0.2pp are not followed by negative SPX 20d returns at least 55% of the time, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO indicates improving industrial production and real activity, which supports earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when production gains are driven by temporary inventory restocking without broad demand improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 2-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative SPX 60d returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the 60d forward-return spread versus flat/declining INDPRO is not positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates simultaneous consumer spending and payroll growth, which confirms broad demand expansion and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect requires both series to improve together; either one alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone for predicting positive SPX 20d returns, or if joint-triggered instances produce negative returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST after a prior high level signals housing-demand weakening and tighter interest-sensitive spending, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The short horizon reflects fast repricing of cyclical growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drops more than 8% below its 3-month average but SPX 5d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or if the average 5d return is not materially below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a higher UNRATE combined with elevated FEDFUNDS indicates policy is restraining demand faster than employment can adjust, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is regime-dependent and is weakest when policy is easing.
failure_condition: Reject if, during tightening cycles, the UNRATE-FEDFUNDS combination does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered observations yield positive SPX 20d returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive while RSAFS also expands, the economy is simultaneously growing in real output and nominal spending, which should support equities over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if GDP growth is positive but sales stagnate, implying weak final demand and weaker earnings transmission.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise versus their prior observations
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous increases in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive SPX 60d returns better than chance, or if negative 60d returns occur in more than 55% of synchronized-expansion cases.
